steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: A square with side length $r$ has perimeter $4r$. Three squares contribute $3 \times 4r = 12r$ total.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: An equilateral triangle with side length $t$ has perimeter $3t$. Five triangles contribute $5 \times 3t = 15t$ total.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$9r+20t=204$’: Uses 9 and 20 as coefficients; those come from treating squares like 3-sided figures and triangles like 4-sided figures. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘$3r+5t=204$’: Uses the counts of shapes (3 and 5) rather than the perimeters; forgets to multiply by sides per shape. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘$12r+15t=204$’: $12r$ = 3 squares × 4 sides each; $15t$ = 5 triangles × 3 sides each. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘$5r+3t=204$’: Swaps the counts entirely; has no relationship to the geometry of the problem. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
